questions.question: What is the process concept in operating systems?
options: A) A module within an operating system
B) A program that performs a specific task
C) A sequence of instructions to perform a task
D) A type of operating system
questions.explanation: A sequence of instructions to perform a task, a process is a program in execution

questions.question: What is the primary function of a process in an operating system?
questions.explanation: To execute a program, a process runs a program in the operating system

questions.question: What is the difference between a process and a thread?
options: A) A process has its own memory space
B) A process has its own CPU time
C) A process is lighter in weight than a thread
D) A thread is lighter in weight than a process
questions.explanation: A process has its own memory space, whereas a thread shares the same memory space as other threads in the same process

questions.question: What happens to a process when it completes its execution?
options: A) It becomes a new process
questions.explanation: When a process completes its execution, it is terminated and its memory is released back to the system

questions.question: What is the purpose of process management in an operating system?
questions.explanation: To manage processes, which involves creating, scheduling, and terminating processes in an operating system
